notes: Recording police in public is generally allowed in Japan as long as it does not interfere with their duties.
Home CCTV is generally legal in Japan for security purposes, but privacy laws apply if cameras capture others.
Employee monitoring is allowed in Japan but must be reasonable and respect worker privacy rights.
Audio recording is generally legal in Japan, including recording conversations you are part of, but misuse can still lead to legal issues.
Recording video in public is generally allowed in Japan, but privacy and harassment laws still apply.
Recording phone calls is generally legal in Japan if you are a participant in the conversation, but misuse can create legal risks.
GPS tracking in Japan can be legal in some contexts, but tracking someone without consent may violate privacy laws.
Drone use is regulated in Japan, and using drones for surveillance is restricted depending on location and permissions.
Businesses in Japan can use CCTV for security, but must comply with data protection and privacy expectations.
Hidden cameras are generally illegal in Japan when used to secretly record individuals, especially in private spaces.
